title: North West Wales 869.618 MHz, BW 62.5 SF8 CR 8

messages.author: NaturalApparatus
messages.text: After seeing how effective the mesh has been running in the south central area on a recent trip, I'll be putting up some Meshcore repeaters here in north Wales. Area mostly Caernarfon, Gwynedd, and Northern Anglesey. Anyone else in the area?
messages.content_html: After seeing how effective the mesh has been running in the south central area on a recent trip, I'll be putting up some Meshcore repeaters here in north Wales. Area mostly Caernarfon, Gwynedd, and Northern Anglesey. Anyone else in the area?

messages.author: pHr0
messages.text: Hey - I'm in N Wales, but right on the Chester border. I've just put up a fairly large +12 db OMNI and am now routing traffic from WInterhill across towards this area, connecting quite a few people. (edited)

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: hi... im new to mesh- well anything mesh /LoRa tbh... but im also based in caernarfon! i wasnt expecting to find many in north wales - never mind "near caernarfon"... ive just ordered my first ever device last night... so come febuary im hoping to finally have a play with meshcore with the hope of building some solar nodes to deploy around the area during the summer! ive got some good ideas i think of where we could get some decent repeaters... i used to live at the marquess of anglesey column years ago... if we could get one up there - it would be seen for miles! The obvious dream here would be the nebo transmitter - although that almost certainly a fever dream! Cool anyway , just saying hello really - looking forward to making my first contacts on the mesh!
messages.content_html: hi... im new to mesh- well anything mesh /LoRa tbh... but im also based in caernarfon! i wasnt expecting to find many in north wales - never mind "near caernarfon"... ive just ordered my first ever device last night... so come febuary im hoping to finally have a play with meshcore with the hope of building some solar nodes to deploy around the area during the summer! ive got some good ideas i think of where we could get some decent repeaters... i used to live at the marquess of anglesey column years ago... if we could get one up there - it would be seen for miles! The obvious dream here would be the nebo transmitter - although that almost certainly a fever dream! Cool anyway , just saying hello really - looking forward to making my first contacts on the mesh!
messages.reply_html:
NaturalApparatus
Northern Anglesey... For the repeater. Home is near Caernarfon

messages.author: NaturalApparatus
messages.text: Hi Keegan, sounds good to me... There's a couple of us in the area. I've been too busy with work and winter to build any more nodes but I do have a recipe that I use and seems to work ok... Though the one at lynas is very busy relaying and the battery sometimes goes - I need a hardware reboot switch on the enclosure. Feel free to DM me or post on this thread! Nebo would be amazing... The local amateur radio repeater GB3AR used to be there but got moved as fees for the site were high. If say one in the area of Nebo or rhosgadfan maybe would be amazing!
messages.content_html: Hi Keegan, sounds good to me... There's a couple of us in the area. The obvious dream here would be the nebo transmitter - although that almost certainly a fever dream! Cool anyway , just saying hello really - looking forward to making my first contacts on the mesh!

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: yeah im currently working out the parts needed to make a set and forget repeater - from my limited research it seems that all you need is a low power nrf board of some sort (rak), solar panel in the range of 25w+ and a load of 18650 cells... the only thing I'm not sure of at the moment is what boards offer ota updating - just incase the whole hobby flips to a new protocol like were seeing with the switch from meshtastic to meshcore... tbh its still early days for me - now ive got a device on the way , ill be taking research and learning a bit more seriously so im sure ill get to grips with it soon enough... Nebo would deffinately be fantastic... if not nebo transmitter itself though... i would be cool to maybe just find some strategic spots up mountains to deploy some nodes... and yeah rhosgad would be ideal... I know a girl from back in college who has a pretty open view from her house up there! same with clynnog fawr aswell... theye all on that curved coastline so i think with good placement - you could expand the mesh quite far with just a few nodes like that... I know the dude who owns the highest property in Rhiwlas which is fairly high once your up there - can easily see holyhead , the orme etc... he would probably let me install a node up there... haha at this rate i may have to set up a gofundme to help pay for some nodes! its alot to think about - but theres some options and deffo some scope for some fun at the very least
messages.content_html: yeah im currently working out the parts needed to make a set and forget repeater - from my limited research it seems that all you need is a low power nrf board of some sort (rak), solar panel in the range of 25w+ and a load of 18650 cells... the only thing I'm not sure of at the moment is what boards offer ota updating - just incase the whole hobby flips to a new protocol like were seeing with the switch from meshtastic to meshcore... tbh its still early days for me - now ive got a device on the way , ill be taking research and learning a bit more seriously so im sure ill get to grips with it soon enough... I know the dude who owns the highest property in Rhiwlas which is fairly high once your up there - can easily see holyhead , the orme etc... he would probably let me install a node up there... haha at this rate i may have to set up a gofundme to help pay for some nodes! its alot to think about - but theres some options and deffo some scope for some fun at the very least

NaturalApparatus
Hi Keegan, sounds good to me... There's a couple of us in the area. I've been too busy with work and winter to build any more nodes but I do have a recipe that I use and seems to work ok... Though the one at lynas is very busy relaying and the battery sometimes goes - I need a hardware reboot switch on the enclosure. Feel free to DM me or post on this thread! Nebo would be amazing... The local amateur radio repeater GB3AR used to be there but got moved as fees for the site were high. If say one in the area of Nebo or rhosgadfan maybe would be amazing!

messages.author: MMTB
messages.text: Multiple repeaters at 330M over looking the Irish Sea. The Mesh is taking off on the East coast here. It would be good to get a permanent link to Wales. I have reached Cumbria and Scotland in a single hop from the high repeaters during lift conditions in the past.
messages.content_html: Multiple repeaters at 330M over looking the Irish Sea. The Mesh is taking off on the East coast here. It would be good to get a permanent link to Wales. I have reached Cumbria and Scotland in a single hop from the high repeaters during lift conditions in the past.

Chris

messages.author: Chris
messages.text: Don’t know for certain but just had pings from a repeater that the only ID letsmesh.net could report was in Holyhead. Also has since died down, so atmospheric conditions have likely changed and it’s not as easy to get signal.
messages.content_html: Don’t know for certain but just had pings from a repeater that the only ID letsmesh.net could report was in Holyhead. Also has since died down, so atmospheric conditions have likely changed and it’s not as easy to get signal.

messages.author: Chris
messages.text: Set it up as a repeater first - it signals to other folks who try out meshcore nearby that there’s someone else so they stick around and you might actually get a ping
messages.content_html: Set it up as a repeater first - it signals to other folks who try out meshcore nearby that there’s someone else so they stick around and you might actually get a ping

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: i think it already is set up as a repeater ya know... its not using normal meshcore from the meshcore.flasher - but rather whisper os... i think and tbh i could be wrong cos i am a bit blagged by it all haha... but there is a feature i turned on called packet forward- i think this kinda makes it a repeater. i know there isnt a dedicated repeater firmware ready to go for it like with other devices
messages.content_html: i think it already is set up as a repeater ya know... its not using normal meshcore from the meshcore.flasher - but rather whisper os... i think and tbh i could be wrong cos i am a bit blagged by it all haha... but there is a feature i turned on called packet forward- i think this kinda makes it a repeater. i know there isnt a dedicated repeater firmware ready to go for it like with other devices

Skynet
Skynet - Local MeshCore community group serving Holyhead, Anglesey, UK. Connect with us through decentralized mesh networking.

messages.author: Chris
messages.text: are you also shipping mqtt data to letsmesh.net ? we're using that to aggregate data in Ireland (north and south) and be great to see if our packets that are reaching dublin are crossing over to holyhead. I see my repeaters up north (bhd-lisburn-observer) got picked up a few days back on skynet (edited)

messages.author: MMTB
messages.text: I picked up an advert at 8AM on the 9th April. If your location can see the Irish sea, we could establish a good link to Ireland as we will have direct line of sight. The current link to Hollyhead 1 is intermittent. If you can see the repeaters in Wicklow, you will connect to Northern Ireland and to the UK via Cumbria.
